title: Theory Regarding the Soul of Unit 00 Quite Complex
display_title: Theory Regarding the Soul of Unit 00 Quite Complex
wikitext:
[]: Theory Regarding the Soul of Unit 00 (Quite Complex)             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Sat Apr 06, 2013 9:39 am This is just the theory I have posted in the Discussion section on the Unit 00's Soul page on the wiki, posted here at someone else's advice. Please remember it is only a theory. I have a personal theory regarding the soul of Unit 00. It is only a theory, but I believe it has some validity. Rei 1 and Rei 2 were made at the same time. Lilith's soul was split into 2 portions: One, which would go into Rei 2,consisted of all the parts of Lilith's personality that would make her good for piloting, obedience, loyalty, etc. This left Rei 2 as the ideal pilot; completely obedient, and totally dependent on her superiors. The unwanted aspects of Lilith's soul, such as her independence, rebelliousness, and passion, were separated from Rei 2's soul fragment, and put into Rei 1. Rei 1 was purely a placeholder until Unit 00 was completed so the soul could be uploaded to Unit 00. The folks behind Unit 00 reasoned that seeing as Unit 00 needed a soul, and the personality of the soul was going to be completely surpressed anyway, that they could in effect use Unit 00 as a lockbox for the unwanted parts of Lilith's soul. Unfortunately, things did not go to plan. Rei 1, possessing the rebellious, scheming, emotional parts of Lilith, was not amused by the casual vivisection of her soul. As such, she tried to get Naoko Akagi "on side" so to speak, and attempted to pit her against Gendo with the "You old hag" speech. Unfortunately for her, this did not work, and Naoko strangled her to death before committing suicide. This was a major blow to Gendo's plans, and Rei 1's soul fragment was hastily uploaded to Unit 00 before it could reunite with the rest of Lilith's soul in Rei 2. Eventually, the activation test was carried out. However, this is where things go pear-shaped. Rei 1's soul fragment, now inside Unit 00, was quite heavily warped by the previous events. In addition to her last memories being a horrible death at the hands of Naoko Akagi, coupled with the already troublesome nature of the soul fragment and the hasty upload, Rei 1/Unit 00's soul had become murderously insane. The combined stress of the aforementioned trauma, the proximity of Rei 2 (and thus the rest of her soul) and the sight of people she hated, resulted in Rei 1/Unit 00 going berserk, attempting to kill Gendo and Ritsuko (possibly mistaking Ritsuko for Naoko) and Rei 2. It is possible that she tried to kill Rei 2 in an attempt to free Rei 2's soul fragment and make her soul complete once more. Unit 00 was thankfully subdued, although not before Rei 2 was heavily injured. Eventually, Rei 1/Unit 00 got used to the presence of Rei 2 inside her, and became comfortable with her. During the cross-compatibility tests, when Shinji tries to sync with Unit 00, Rei 1/Unit 00 does not recognize him (hence the "bug-eyed Rei" scene). Enraged at the presence of someone other than Rei 2 trying to sync with her, she goes berserk again, attempting to remove the unwanted presence of Shinji from within her. Throughout the rest of the series, Unit 00 functions without further mishap, right up until episode 23, when Amrisael attempts to fuse with Unit 00 to resurrect its fellow angels. Amrisael encounters both Rei 1 and 2, leading up to the conversation scene. Rei 2 then initiates the self-destruct sequence, vapourizing Unit 00 and Rei 2. With Unit 00 now a large red stain on the ground, and Rei 2 now Kentucky Fried Rei, both Rei 1 and Rei 2's soul fragments are free, and are uploaded to Rei 3 by the cloning plant. However, now that both soul fragments are in the same body, Lilith's soul is now once again complete. Rei 2's love for Shinji, her memories and knowledge, combined with Rei 1's anger, hatred of Gendo, rebelliousness and so on, lead Rei 3/Lilith to betray Gendo in End of Evangelion, rejecting his plans and handing over control of Instrumentality to the person she loves, Shinji (This is kinda awesome on Shinji's part if you think about it: He made the creator of the human race, effectively god, fall in love with him!). Also (warning: this next section is pure conjecture on my part), when Unit 01 is absorbed into the Tree of Life, and subsequently into Lilith/Rei, both Lilith/Rei and Yui have a common goal: revenge on Gendo, in Lilith/Rei's case for defiling her soul and making her his pawn, in Yui's case for mistreating her beloved son. As such, Lilith/Rei allows Yui to manifest before Gendo as a daemonic Unit 01, and uses their combined powers to utterly destroy him (represented by his consumption by Unit 01), cutting him out of Instrumentality and forever denying him his plans to be reunited with Yui. Of course, like I say, it's just a theory, but hey, I tried.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Sat Apr 06, 2013 11:00 am I like parts of this. For instance, It's been speculated before that the reason Rei 1 taunts Naoko is that part of her soul had already been split into Unit-00 leaving her a little crazy. I think that the idea that part of her soul was already in Rei 2 is new, but it works just as well to explain Rei 1's behavior. I also like the notion that the knowledge of what had been done to her that Rei 3 got from Rei 1 helps explain her behavior. It's usually just stated that the change in personality that came with her complete soul (minus whatever sliver welcomes her home in EOE) is what prompts her to rebel against Gendo. Or else she was just angry that he wouldn't let her die. The idea that the knowledge she gained when her soul was reunited triggered her rebellion is an improvement on that. But other parts raise questions that need an answer. Why the Rei 1 stage at all? Why not put the first fragment directly into Unit-00? Are we assuming that Unit-00 wasn't ready? Also, we need to remember that Gendo has been showing Rei 1 around; it seems that she is Rei's "public face." Why would they make the Rei with the potential for independence and rebellion the one who runs around loose in Nerv HQ? Wouldn't they keep the potential trouble maker locked up and make the obedient pilot candidate Rei the one who interacts with the personnel? Besides, Rei 2 would eventually have been the one people knew when she started piloting. Wouldn't people have noticed the drastic personality change when Rei 1 went into Unit-00 and Rei 2 took her place at Nerv? It would seem to be a lot safer for Gendo to introduce Rei 2 to everyone if she were available.

[]:             Quote Post by Paranoid » Sat Apr 06, 2013 11:13 am thewayneiac wrote: But other parts raise questions that need an answer. Why the Rei 1 stage at all? Why not put the first fragment directly into Unit-00? Are we assuming that Unit-00 wasn't ready?

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sat Apr 06, 2013 7:42 pm I think that the Rei II to Rei III transition most heavily suggests that only one "Rei Prime" exists at a time. Naoko even says as she kills Rei I that Rei can simply be replaced. Moreover, the show further suggests that the Rei "replacement parts" are being grown in the Reiquarium. However, this thread does raise the old question of why Rei I has such a different personality to Rei II and indeed Rei III. It is clear that Nerv have somehow managed to get Rei "under control" in the meantime, but the method isn't very clear. Perhaps this has something to do with the medication Rei is always taking? But before looking at this, or Unit-00, what about looking again at the dummy Rei's in the Requarium, and their counterparts, the Transition guide Rei-lets/Rei-tachi(what did we call these again?) in EoE. These odd shards of Rei are always smiling, laughing, giggling, and generally acting in a most un-Rei-like fashion. Could Nerv be purging Rei's emotions out of her during her trips to the Reiquarium, just as they purged out the negative aspects of Rei I from her which somehow ended up in Unit-00? Maybe they learned something from the Unit-02 contact experiment; something useful. 1. Yui's soul was transferred into unit 01 before Rei was cloned. If the unit numbers are anything to go by, unit 00 should have existed at this time.

[]:             Quote Post by Paranoid » Sat Apr 06, 2013 8:03 pm I agree with you about there being only one Rei prime. Regarding unit 00, though, I'm not sure I agree. It's true that they failed at making evas plenty of times in the past, but I don't think they'd move on from unit 00 to unit 01 (in terms of numbering, not necessarily in terms of actually creating the eva) before actually getting a working unit 00. If they'd gotten 01 to working order before 00, wouldn't they call it 00? On the other hand, it's not entirely improbable given that 00 and 01 come from different seeds of life. Maybe 00 and 01 referred more to production lines than the units themselves, with the final (successful) model of each production line being given that number as designation. This is all highly speculative anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Jul 26, 2013 3:17 am I still think the soul of Unit 00 is an eidolon, or at best part of Rei's divided soul (a la Kyoko and Unit 02). The whole "Rei I is in Unit 00" business, apart from being horribly inelegant, fails to address what's in Rei II. But if Rei II was originally complete and only became the girl we know after a CE with Unit 00 everything falls into place -- the Eva is unstable because it only has an echo of a soul, or has Lilith's more erratic, "unRei-like" tendencies, and Rei II is as she is for the same reason Kyoko became what she became. This would also explain why the soul in Unit 00 appears to be a twisted version of Rei, which doesn't fit with the Rei I hypothesis (which also runs into issues when the Unit 00 soul and Rei I show up onscreen at the same time at the end). No soul storing, no handwaving, just the established mechanisms of the show applied in different fashions.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Fri Jul 26, 2013 3:44 am I tend to think that the part of Rei's soul that contained her memories of being Lilith or her identity as a Seed of Life was somehow split, possibly when she was created, or possibly was planned all along. Note that while Kaworu seems to be fully aware of his identity as Adam and his powers, as well as seemingly possessing enough of Adam's knowledge to identify Lilith and knowing about Third Impact and such, Rei II has no such knowledge at the ability of the series. Instead, Rei III somehow seems to slowly realise that she is Lilith over the course of Episode 24, which culminates in her regaining or realising her ATF powers at the end. My theory is that Rei II was intentionally separated from the portion of her soul that identified her as Lilith in order to prevent her from realising her true power and possibly going against the wishes of Gendo and SEELE. Notably it is always Rei I who seems to be the most Lilith-like of the three Reis, such as in the TV Episode 24 and 25. In the NGE2 game the soul in Eva-00 seems to contact Rei at one point and seems to possess enough of Lilith's knowledge to tell Rei that she must "Retrieve the Fruit of Life from Adam", although Rei herself doesn't seem to know anything about either the Fruit of Life or Adam at this point. After Rei II and Eva-00 were destroyed, Rei's soul regained the "Lilith" portion that had been placed into Eva-00, allowing her to regain Lilith's knowledge and powers.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Mon Jul 29, 2013 6:30 pm Bagheera wrote: (which also runs into issues when the Unit 00 soul and Rei I show up onscreen at the same time at the end)

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Mon Jul 29, 2013 6:54 pm There's a shot of a spectral Rei I, II, and III in EoE. It's not unreasonable to assume that the child-Rei scene with the others is meant to be the soul in Unit 00, but that's not correct- this is not a literal image but a commentary on the Eva's function, but a recurrence of the Maid-Matron-Crone imagery that pervades Eva, and EoE in particular. The young Rei is Rei I. Unit 00 doesn't have a soul. Nor do the MPEs, and probably not 03 or 04. While it's never explicitly stated, one of the goals of the Eva program is an Evangelion that is remotely controlled and requires neither pilot nor resident soul. The Dummy Plug, the cross-synch tests with Shinji and Rei in one another's Evas, etc. are all meant to facilitate this end. Ultimately Nerv was incapable of achieving it- what they needed was an Eva created from the flesh of Adam and a way to imitate Adam's soul the way Adam did with Lilith, i.e. a dummy plug based on Kaworu as opposed to one based on Rei. This may well be the explanation Gendo gave Kaji for stealing Adam from Seele, assuming he offered any explanation at all- but that's pure conjecture. This is stated unequivocally in the show- Kaworu can remotely control Unit 02, and he explicitly says that this is not because of Kyoko's soul, but in spite of it , as she has "shut [herself] away for now". Further evidence is found in Episode 19, when Yui rejects the Dummy Plug- despite being a copy or imitation of Lilith, the Dummy Plug can't simply override Yui and must be permitted to activate the system. Besides the absurdities of logistics of collecting and storing souls for Eva production, and the lack of an internal consistent candidate for Unit 00's soul, and the implication that Nerv has some means of transferring souls into Evas during production, the idea that every single Eva must have a soul to operate flies in the face of what Nerv is attempting to do- build an Eva that doesn't need a pilot. An ensouled Eva must have a pilot , and not just any pilot, but one specific one. As further evidence for this argument, I posit the question- what if Unit 01, one of the only two Evas that is explicitly confirmed to have a soul, didn't have one? Assuming that Rei, under the correct circumstances, could control the flesh of Lilith as Kaworu did the flesh of Adam, the Dummy Plug would simply have worked and gone off to fight Zeruel. Hence why Unit 00 and Unit 03 are tested - while Unit 01 and Unit 02 are just switched on. Nerv knows the ensouled Evas will work, 00 and 03 are experiments. They have no souls. The pilot candidates are candidates because of their age, not because of any personal connection to the Evas. They're orphans because their emotional states will allow them to become dependent on the Eva, to "open their heart" to it and be able to pilot. The resident soul idea doesn't make a lick of sense, and misses the point of Yui and Kyoko's fates.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Jul 29, 2013 6:54 pm Monk Ed wrote: What? Where?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Jul 30, 2013 1:08 am Bagheera wrote: EoTV.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Jul 30, 2013 1:22 am NemZ wrote: How is that not the same as Rei 1? I'm confused.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Jul 30, 2013 1:56 am I just watched the scene I think you're referring to and didn't notice that. Can you be more specific?

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Jul 30, 2013 5:53 am NemZ wrote: I just watched the scene I think you're referring to and didn't notice that. Can you be more specific?

[]:             Quote Post by Atropos » Tue Jul 30, 2013 6:25 am You're the first person I've seen say this but it looks like you're right. The Rei in Eva-00 shows up in EoTV, and Rei I is speaking at the same time, but there's no indication that they're meant to be the same character, as Rei I said something earlier and looked completely different. Considering that's one of the major pieces of evidence for the 'Rei I in Unit-00' theory, I'm surprised this hasn't been brought up already by its detractors.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Jul 30, 2013 7:13 am If Rei1 is talking and Rei00 is on the screen, shouldn't that rather be taken as confirmation that they are in fact the same? Then again the whole point of the scene is to show that all the Reis we see throughout the show are coalescing into a single Rei. That said, if Rei1 became split into Rei00 and Rei2 then in a way they must be distinct just as Rei1 is distinct from Rei2 and Rei3, and all are distinct from Lilith.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Jul 30, 2013 8:05 am Atropos wrote: You're the first person I've seen say this but it looks like you're right. The Rei in Eva-00 shows up in EoTV, and Rei I is speaking at the same time, but there's no indication that they're meant to be the same character, as Rei I said something earlier and looked completely different. Considering that's one of the major pieces of evidence for the 'Rei I in Unit-00' theory, I'm surprised this hasn't been brought up already by its detractors.

[]:             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Tue Jul 30, 2013 8:34 am Having thought over it, I have made a slight alteration to my theory. It's fundamentally the same, but with a different theory on Rei herself. Rei 1 housed Lilith's complete soul, including the rebellious parts aforementioned. Initially it was simply planned that she would be used as the pilot for 00, and eventually as part of Instrumentality. At the time, a suitable soul had not been found for Zerogouki. Naturally, she was not too happy about this and attempted to turn Naoko against Gendo but failed and was killed, yadda yadda yadda. However, Gendo and co actually found out how Rei 1 had died, they quickly extracted Rei 1's soul and then split it. It was vivisected into 2 parts; Rei 2, containing the useful parts of Lilith's soul, thus making sure that Rei 2 was obedient, loyal, and thus not going to turn against the group again. The remaining parts, her independence, emotions, scheming capability, etc, were then used as the soul for Unit 00-It was assumed by GEHIRN-now NERV-that this would both provide a highly convenient soul and also lock away Rei 1's undesireable traits within a fully controlled body. Unfortunately this did not go according to plan. Rei 00, being composed solely of the parts of Rei 1 that NERV Did not need, was a violently insane mess of a soul, and as such when Unit 00 was activated the Evangelion went totally berserk, desperately trying to reunite with the rest of her soul by killing Rei 2, and also attempting to kill her tormentors, the NERV team, who she recognized behind the window. Eventually she got used to the presence of Rei 2 in proximity to her, but when Shinji attempted to sync with her she hated the presence of a stranger's soul and once again went berserk. When Unit 00 and Rei 2 were both destroyed in the battle against Amrisael, and Rei 3 was created, Rei 2's soul reunited with Rei 00, completing Lilith's soul-essentially Rei 1. The combined force of Rei 2's memories and love for Shinji with Rei 00's previously locked away emotions and the experiences of Rei 1 (before Naoko murdered her) and Rei 00 are what lead her to betray Gendo in EOE, blah blah, basically the same as the previous iteration of the theory. I don't buy the idea that Unit 00 has no soul. Rei had no control or access to her powers until EOE, unlike Kaworu who was basically an Angel taking the form of a human. She still needed a soul to sync with to pilot an Eva. Likewise, Shinji is able to sync with Unit 00, although it goes berserk in the process. somethin that couldn't happen without a soul.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Tue Jul 30, 2013 11:52 am Squigsquasher wrote: Rei had no control or access to her powers until EOE, unlike Kaworu who was basically an Angel taking the form of a human.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Fri Aug 02, 2013 3:43 pm If anyone doubts that Eva-00 has a soul or that Rei I is not that soul, here's the smoking gun from Episode #23. Rei senses the presence of the invading angel, and at first mistakes it for "the me inside the Eva". Episode #23' wrote: [wkimg]23_121.jpg[/wkimg] Rei: Who's there? Me? The me inside the Eva. No, I can sense that there's someone other than me. Who are you? An Angel? The person that we call an Angel?

The whole "Rei I is in Unit 00" business, apart from being horribly inelegant, fails to address what's in Rei II. But if Rei II was originally complete and only became the girl we know after a CE with Unit 00 everything falls into place -- the Eva is unstable because it only has an echo of a soul, or has Lilith's more erratic, "unRei-like" tendencies, and Rei II is as she is for the same reason Kyoko became what she became. This would also explain why the soul in Unit 00 appears to be

Article: Theory Regarding the Soul of Unit 00 Quite Complex
 soul, or has Lilith's more erratic, "unRei-like" tendencies, and Rei II is as she is for the same reason Kyoko became what she became. This would also explain why the soul in Unit 00 appears to be a twisted version of Rei, which doesn't fit with the Rei I hypothesis (which also runs into issues when the Unit 00 soul and Rei I show up onscreen at the same time at the end). No soul storing, no handwaving, just the established mechanisms of the show applied in different fashions.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Fri Jul 26, 2013 3:44 am I tend to think that the part of Rei's soul that contained her memories of being Lilith or her identity as a Seed of Life was somehow split, possibly when she was created, or possibly was planned all along. Note that while Kaworu seems to be fully aware of his identity as Adam and his powers, as well as seemingly possessing enough of Adam's knowledge to identify Lilith and knowing about Third Impact and such, Rei II has no such knowledge at the ability of the series. Instead, Rei III somehow seems to slowly realise that she is Lilith over the course of Episode 24, which culminates in her regaining or realising her ATF powers at the end. My theory is that Rei II was intentionally separated from the portion of her soul that identified her as Lilith in order to prevent her from realising her true power and possibly going against the wishes of Gendo and SEELE. Notably it is always Rei I who seems to be the most Lilith-like of the three Reis, such as in the TV Episode 24 and 25. In the NGE2 game the soul in Eva-00 seems to contact Rei at one point and seems to possess enough of Lilith's knowledge to tell Rei that she must "Retrieve the Fruit of Life from Adam", although Rei herself doesn't seem to know anything about either the Fruit of Life or Adam at this point. After Rei II and Eva-00 were destroyed, Rei's soul regained the "Lilith" portion that had been placed into Eva-00, allowing her to regain Lilith's knowledge and powers.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Mon Jul 29, 2013 6:54 pm There's a shot of a spectral Rei I, II, and III in EoE. It's not unreasonable to assume that the child-Rei scene with the others is meant to be the soul in Unit 00, but that's not correct- this is not a literal image but a commentary on the Eva's function, but a recurrence of the Maid-Matron-Crone imagery that pervades Eva, and EoE in particular. The young Rei is Rei I. Unit 00 doesn't have a soul. Nor do the MPEs, and probably not 03 or 04. While it's never explicitly stated, one of the goals of the Eva program is an Evangelion that is remotely controlled and requires neither pilot nor resident soul. The Dummy Plug, the cross-synch tests with Shinji and Rei in one another's Evas, etc. are all meant to facilitate this end. Ultimately Nerv was incapable of achieving it- what they needed was an Eva created from the flesh of Adam and a way to imitate Adam's soul the way Adam did with Lilith, i.e. a dummy plug based on Kaworu as opposed to one based on Rei. This may well be the explanation Gendo gave Kaji for stealing Adam from Seele, assuming he offered any explanation at all- but that's pure conjecture. This is stated unequivocally in the show- Kaworu can remotely control Unit 02, and he explicitly says that this is not because of Kyoko's soul, but in spite of it , as she has "shut [herself] away for now". Further evidence is found in Episode 19, when Yui rejects the Dummy Plug- despite being a copy or imitation of Lilith, the Dummy Plug can't simply override Yui and must be permitted to activate the system. Besides the absurdities of logistics of collecting and storing souls for Eva production, and the lack of an internal consistent candidate for Unit 00's soul, and the implication that Nerv has some means of transferring souls into Evas during production, the idea that every single Eva must

Article: Theory Regarding the Soul of Unit 00 Quite Complex
 logistics of collecting and storing souls for Eva production, and the lack of an internal consistent candidate for Unit 00's soul, and the implication that Nerv has some means of transferring souls into Evas during production, the idea that every single Eva must have a soul to operate flies in the face of what Nerv is attempting to do- build an Eva that doesn't need a pilot. An ensouled Eva must have a pilot , and not just any pilot, but one specific one. As further evidence for this argument, I posit the question- what if Unit 01, one of the only two Evas that is explicitly confirmed to have a soul, didn't have one? Assuming that Rei, under the correct circumstances, could control the flesh of Lilith as Kaworu did the flesh of Adam, the Dummy Plug would simply have worked and gone off to fight Zeruel. Hence why Unit 00 and Unit 03 are tested - while Unit 01 and Unit 02 are just switched on. Nerv knows the ensouled Evas will work, 00 and 03 are experiments. They have no souls. The pilot candidates are candidates because of their age, not because of any personal connection to the Evas. They're orphans because their emotional states will allow them to become dependent on the Eva, to "open their heart" to it and be able to pilot. The resident soul idea doesn't make a lick of sense, and misses the point of Yui and Kyoko's fates.

Article: Theory Regarding the Soul of Unit 00 Quite Complex
 by Squigsquasher » Tue Jul 30, 2013 8:34 am Having thought over it, I have made a slight alteration to my theory. It's fundamentally the same, but with a different theory on Rei herself. Rei 1 housed Lilith's complete soul, including the rebellious parts aforementioned. Initially it was simply planned that she would be used as the pilot for 00, and eventually as part of Instrumentality. At the time, a suitable soul had not been found for Zerogouki. Naturally, she was not too happy about this and attempted to turn Naoko against Gendo but failed and was killed, yadda yadda yadda. However, Gendo and co actually found out how Rei 1 had died, they quickly extracted Rei 1's soul and then split it. It was vivisected into 2 parts; Rei 2, containing the useful parts of Lilith's soul, thus making sure that Rei 2 was obedient, loyal, and thus not going to turn against the group again. The remaining parts, her independence, emotions, scheming capability, etc, were then used as the soul for Unit 00-It was assumed by GEHIRN-now NERV-that this would both provide a highly convenient soul and also lock away Rei 1's undesireable traits within a fully controlled body. Unfortunately this did not go according to plan. Rei 00, being composed solely of the parts of Rei 1 that NERV Did not need, was a violently insane mess of a soul, and as such when Unit 00 was activated the Evangelion went totally berserk, desperately trying to reunite with the rest of her soul by killing Rei 2, and also attempting to kill her tormentors, the NERV team, who she recognized behind the window. Eventually she got used to the presence of Rei 2 in proximity to her, but when Shinji attempted to sync with her she hated the presence of a stranger's soul and once again went berserk. When Unit 00 and Rei 2 were both destroyed in the battle against Amrisael, and Rei 3 was created, Rei 2's soul reunited with Rei 00, completing Lilith's soul-essentially Rei 1. The combined force of Rei 2's memories and love for Shinji with Rei 00's previously locked away emotions and the experiences of Rei 1 (before Naoko murdered her) and Rei 00 are what lead her to

Article: Theory Regarding the Soul of Unit 00 Quite Complex
 Rei 1. The combined force of Rei 2's memories and love for Shinji with Rei 00's previously locked away emotions and the experiences of Rei 1 (before Naoko murdered her) and Rei 00 are what lead her to betray Gendo in EOE, blah blah, basically the same as the previous iteration of the theory. I don't buy the idea that Unit 00 has no soul. Rei had no control or access to her powers until EOE, unlike Kaworu who was basically an Angel taking the form of a human. She still needed a soul to sync with to pilot an Eva. Likewise, Shinji is able to sync with Unit 00, although it goes berserk in the process. somethin that couldn't happen without a soul.
